resources.string: Everyone is equal before the law without distinction as to language, race, color, sex, political opinion, philosophical belief, religion and sect, or any such grounds.
It is essential to take necessary measures to ensure that people with disabilities can access social, economic, and cultural life equally. Discrimination is prohibited.
Everyone has the right to access healthcare services. You can receive free emergency care at public hospitals regardless of your status.
Everyone is treated equally. No one is discriminated against.
People with disabilities have the same rights to join all parts of life.
Everyone can get medical help when they are sick.
Everyone has the right to respect for his or her private and family life, his or her home and his or her correspondence.
Your private life must be respected. No one can learn your private information without permission.
Everyone has the right to work, to free choice of employment, to just and favourable conditions of work and to protection against unemployment.
Everyone has the right to work. The government helps you find a job and protects your rights.
Every citizen has the right and the opportunity to vote and to be elected at genuine periodic elections.
When you are old enough, you can vote or be a candidate. It is your right to join elections.
